channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Here's what to expect from Friday's release of the April jobs report
item.description: The total picture is one of a labor market that, while undoubtedly cooling, is generally stable and resilient despite a number of challenges.

item.title: Trump threatens EU with ‘much higher’ tariffs if no trade deal signed by new deadline
item.description: President Donald Trump said he will give the European Union until July 4 to ratify its trade agreement with the U.S.

item.title: Toyota fourth-quarter profit misses by wide margin as U.S. tariffs drive 49% slump
item.description: The world's largest automaker by sales volume saw a 1.89% year-on-year rise in revenue during the fourth quarter ended March.

item.title: Nintendo hikes Switch 2 prices and expects console sales to decline as memory crunch bites
item.description: Nintendo said the price of the Switch 2 in the U.S. will rise from $449.99 to $499.99. In Japan, Nintendo will hike the price from 49,980 yen to 59,980 yen.

item.title: These banks just raised their CD rates. Where to find the highest yields
item.description: Morgan Stanley expects rates on certificates of deposit to be flat to slightly higher in the near term.

item.title: Nvidia CEO says AI partnership with Corning will 'revitalize American manufacturing'
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang highlighted his company's partnership with Corning as an opportunity to reinvest in American manufacturing.

item.title: French prosecutors escalate probe of Elon Musk and X to criminal investigation
item.description: France is investigating whether Elon Musk and his social media app X were complicit in spreading harmful AI deepfakes.

item.title: Sony targets double-digit profit growth despite slowdown in PlayStation 5 sales amid memory price crunch
item.description: While hardware sales took a hit in Sony's fourth-quarter, strong revenue from its image sensing and music businesses offset the dip.

item.title: Redistricting race to the bottom ramps up, as GOP eyes more states to draw new House maps
item.description: An April Supreme Court ruling has caused a scrambled in some southern states to redistrict before November's midterm elections.

item.title: Oil prices fall below $100 as Trump insists ceasefire still intact after U.S.-Iran exchange fire
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz, raising fresh fears that the fragile ceasefire between the two countries was unraveling.

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz; each claims other shot first
item.description: CENTCOM's statement does not mention the status of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, which has already been frayed by repeated claims that its terms are being breached.

item.title: ‘Not a Chance Hormuz Opens’: How Wall Street’s new NACHO trade bets on a prolonged oil shock
item.description: Traders are embracing the “NACHO” trade, betting Hormuz disruptions and elevated oil prices will persist longer than expected.

item.title: IREN inks AI infrastructure deal with Nvidia
item.description: Data center operator IREN announced a partnership with semiconductor giant Nvidia.

item.title: CoreWeave stock sinks 10% on weak revenue guidance, increased spending forecast
item.description: CoreWeave has been raising debt to finance its data center buildout, and S&P has boosted the company's credit rating.

item.title: Cloudflare stock sinks 18% after earnings as company cuts 1,100 employees due to AI changes
item.description: The cloud provider announced it is laying off 20% of its workforce as agentic artificial intelligence "fundamentally changes" the company's work.

item.title: OpenAI rolls out new model for cybersecurity teams a month after Anthropic's Mythos debut
item.description: OpenAI said GPT-5.5-Cyber, a variation of its latest AI model, is rolling out in a limited preview capacity to vetted cybersecurity teams.

item.title: Iran war market jitters offer silver lining for investors
item.description: Market volatility offers investors a good time to reexamine if their stock allocations align well with their risk tolerance.

item.title: While many international brands retreat, McDonald’s is supersizing its China business
item.description: McDonald's plans to have 10,000 locations in China by the end of 2028, and the country is its second largest market.

item.title: Coinbase posts steep first-quarter loss after slide in crypto prices; shares fall 4%
item.description: Crypto trading platform Coinbase reported a surprise first-quarter loss and weaker-than-estimated revenue after the market close Thursday.

item.title: Corning CEO to Cramer: Deals with 2 unnamed hyperscalers 'larger' than $6B Meta pact
item.description: The investment story for Corning looks even sweeter in the wake of Jim Cramer's sit-down with CEO Wendell Weeks.

item.title: Airbnb tops revenue estimates, but Middle East cancellations rise due to Iran war
item.description: Airbnb reported mixed first-quarter results after the bell on Thursday and warned of regional weakness due to the war in Iran.

item.title: Marriott Bonvoy Business card review: Earn up to 5 free nights, worth up to 250,000 points
item.description: The Marriott Bonvoy Business card is worth keeping for its annual free night and elite status perks.

item.title: Gilt yields ease as UK PM Starmer says 'tough' election results won't make him quit
item.description: The U.K. is counting votes after an election of local councilors and devolved governments on Thursday.

item.title: Tech stocks could offer their best value in years, analysts say, after stellar earnings season
item.description: A succession of strong earnings seasons since then has allowed tech stocks to "grow into" their stock prices.

item.title: France is fan favorite for the FIFA World Cup — but AI is backing another nation for glory, says BofA
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is expected to add over $40 billion to the global GDP with its most lucrative edition ever.

item.title: Billion-dollar bets: Global investors chase Indian Premier League to ride booming cricket economy
item.description: India is the world’s fastest-growing consumer market, and with cricket commanding near-religious devotion, investors are rushing to own IPL franchises.

item.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
item.description: Thanks to the Iran war, big issues for U.S. businesses like rare earths and supply chains may take a back seat on the agenda for the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: Why pension funds are doubling down on private credit despite deepening cracks
item.description: Pension funds are sticking with private credit, and in some cases doubling down even as concerns mount over underwriting standards, valuation opacity and sector concentration.

item.title: Datadog stock soars 31% on blockbuster earnings as AI winners emerge in software
item.description: Datadog's results fueled shares of other cloud infrastructure names like Snowflake and MongoDB.

item.title: Jim Cramer says the AI boom has 'the power to keep the country's economy humming'
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said he's confident in the power of the artificial intelligence boom to keep driving stocks higher, despite short pullbacks.
